key_insight: This paper proves that adaptive FEM can achieve optimal complexity even when driven by non-residual a posteriori estimators and coupled with an iterative linear solver.
engineering_relevance: It supports reliable, efficient mesh-and-solver adaptivity for symmetric elliptic PDE simulations, including common practical estimators such as Zienkiewicz–Zhu and equilibrated flux methods.
method: Abstractly model the a-posteriori estimator and iterative solver.
Analyze the standard adaptive loop for mesh refinement plus algebraic solves.
Prove unconditional full R-linear convergence, and optimal complexity for sufficiently small adaptivity parameters.
evidence_signal: The result is theoretical: the paper establishes convergence and optimality guarantees under abstract assumptions, measured against nonlinear approximation classes and overall computation cost.
watch_item: Optimal complexity is only guaranteed for sufficiently small adaptivity parameters, so parameter choice and estimator assumptions matter in practice.
paper_title: Optimal complexity of adaptive FEM for second-order linear elliptic PDEs driven by non-residual estimators, Part I: Symmetric PDEs
